definition: A proposal by one party expressing willingness to enter into a contract on specific, definite terms. An offer lapses when rejected, when a counteroffer is made, or after a reasonable time. A bid or estimate is not automatically an offer.
An unconditional agreement to all terms of an offer exactly as made. Any modification of terms creates a counteroffer, voids the original offer, and starts a new negotiation.

Something of legal value exchanged by each party to a contract — typically a promise to perform work or a promise to pay. A promise to do something already legally required does not constitute valid consideration.

Failure by one party to perform contractual obligations without legal excuse. A material breach entitles the non-breaching party to treat the contract as terminated and sue for compensatory damages. A minor breach allows damages but not termination.

A written, signed amendment to an existing construction contract that documents a change in scope, price, or schedule. When the original contract is written, change orders must also be in writing and signed by both parties before the changed work begins.

A contractual clause specifying a predetermined amount — often per day — owed if a party fails to perform on time. Enforceable only when the specified amount is a reasonable pre-estimate of actual damages at the time of contracting, not a penalty.

A percentage — typically 5 to 10 percent — of each progress payment withheld by the project owner until substantial completion. Retainage incentivizes the contractor to complete punch-list items and provides a fund to cover latent defects.

An active Oregon Construction Contractors Board license that a contractor must hold before performing compensated construction work or advertising as a contractor. The license number must appear in written contracts and specified advertising media.

The individual designated on a contractor's CCB license application who is responsible for the qualifying examination, maintaining required insurance and bond, and ensuring the business complies with Oregon contractor law under ORS Chapter 701.

A CCB-required disclosure that must be delivered to a residential client before work begins on a project subject to ORS 701.305. OAR 812-012-0130 specifies the required delivery timing and requires proof-of-delivery to be retained.
